target.title: fix: add clear separator between metadata and user message
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a user message is passed to the LLM, the metadata blocks (sender info, conversation info, etc.) and the actual user message content are joined with only double newlines. This causes the LLM to sometimes misinterpret the message structure, treating the metadata as the entire message and ignoring the actual user content ("message is empty" replies).
- **Why it matters:** Users intermittently get incorrect "message is empty" responses even though their message was correctly stored in session history.
- **What changed:**
  - `get-reply-run.ts`: When inbound metadata is present, inject `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator instead of joining with `\n\n`.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.ts`: Add `stripUserMessageSeparator()` post-processing in both `stripInboundMetadata` (webchat/API/gateway) and `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` (TUI) to remove the separator after metadata blocks are stripped. Gated on `didStripMetadata` flag for defense-in-depth.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.test.ts`: 7 new tests covering separator stripping and legacy preservation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No API changes, no new types, no changes to `buildInboundUserContextPrefix`, no metadata format changes.

### Why `---openclaw:user-msg---` instead of `---\n**User Message:**`

The separator is a program-generated marker that **cannot exist** in any legacy stored message or user-typed content. This eliminates the need for complex detection logic (`_sep` markers, `seenUserContent` flags, structural JSON parsing) and makes legacy message compatibility trivial: the stripper only removes `---openclaw:user-msg---`, which legacy data never contains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- LLM now receives a clear `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator between metadata and user content, reducing "message is empty" false positives.
- The separator is stripped in all user-facing surfaces (TUI, webchat, gateway API, session cost logs).
- Legacy messages (pre-upgrade) are completely unaffected — the unique marker cannot appear in historical data.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04)
- Runtime: Node v22.22.1, pnpm 10.23.0

### Steps

1. Send a message to the LLM through any channel with metadata (group chat, sender info, etc.)
2. Observe the prompt construction in `get-reply-run.ts`
3. Verify the separator appears between metadata and user content
4. Verify `stripInboundMetadata` / `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` removes the separator in user-facing views

### Expected

- LLM correctly distinguishes metadata from user message content
- No separator visible in TUI/webchat/gateway/session logs

### Actual

- Confirmed working as expected in unit tests

## Evidence

- [x] Passing tests: 27 strip-inbound-meta tests pass (7 new separator-specific tests)
- New tests cover: separator after single/multiple metadata blocks, no-metadata preservation, non-separator `---` preservation, sentinel-without-metadata-block edge case, legacy message preservation

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: metadata with separator injection, separator stripping after single/multiple metadata blocks, preservation when no metadata present, preservation of `---` in non-separator context
- Edge cases checked: legacy messages with `---\n**User Message:**` user content (preserved intact), sentinel text without metadata fences, user content starting with generic `---`
- What you did **not** verify: Live end-to-end with real LLM provider

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — legacy messages are unaffected; the unique marker cannot appear in pre-upgrade data
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `get-reply-run.ts`, `strip-inbound-meta.ts`
- Known bad symptoms: `---openclaw:user-msg---` appearing in TUI/webchat/session history

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LLM treats `---openclaw:user-msg---` as confusing noise
  - Mitigation: The marker reads like a boundary tag; LLMs routinely handle similar delimiters in system/user prompts. It's a single line and less disruptive than the metadata blocks themselves.

target.title: fix(sessions): honor explicit default model pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist explicit user selections of the configured default model as exact session model pins instead of clearing them as if they were unpinned defaults.
- Keep `/model default`, `session_status(model=default)`, and `sessions.patch model:null` as the explicit clear/unpin paths.
- Carry the exact selection through reply, queued-run, agent-command, reset, status, and sessions.patch paths so channel/parent/config fallback policy cannot silently replace user intent.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Explicit `/model <configured default>` selections no longer lose user intent just because they match the configured primary; fallback policy treats them as strict user session selections, while `/model default` still clears back to unpinned behavior.
Real environment tested: Local beatrice.local source checkout at PR head `0ba67184c403941421ac9e409b592829dd0c224c` on May 23, 2026. Built with `node scripts/build-all.mjs`, then ran a local behavior probe against the built `dist/` session/status modules. No channel credentials or provider API keys were used.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/status/status-message.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts`
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts`
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session-reset-model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",

        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },

  }

  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

target.title: fix: preflight malformed OpenShell exec commands
target.body:
Fixes #72373.

## Summary
- Add a shared SSH exec command preflight before remote `/bin/sh -c` execution.
- Reject unresolved generated placeholders like `<workflow-id>` and common malformed shell input such as unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, unterminated command substitutions, and unterminated here-docs.
- Preserve legitimate shell syntax including redirection, compact redirection, here-doc bodies, process substitution, arithmetic expansion, quoted placeholder-looking text, and comments.
- Build remote exec commands before opening SSH/OpenShell sessions so malformed input fails before session setup.

This follows ClawSweeper's suggested fix shape: keep the existing loop-detection behavior and add the remaining focused validator at the shared SSH/OpenShell exec boundary.

## Proof
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts -t "rejects malformed generated exec commands|allows shell features and quoted placeholder-looking text|uses the shared SSH exec command preflight|rejects malformed exec commands before opening"` -> 22 passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sandbox/ssh.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.ts src/agents/sandbox.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts extensions/openshell/src/cli.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Local Test Note
The broader requested Windows run of `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` still fails on existing Windows-only assumptions unrelated to this patch: path slicing around `config` and symlink creation permissions. The new preflight-focused cases pass.

target.title: fix(usage-cost): surface unpriced-model spend as missingCostEntries instead of a confident $0
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw gateway usage-cost` reports `totalCost: 0` with `missingCostEntries: 0` for models that ship an all-zero `cost` block (e.g. codex `gpt-5.5`, whose Codex backend exposes no per-token price). Real pay-per-token usage is therefore reported as a confident, *complete* `$0`, and every budget / spike / daily-spend safeguard that keys off `totalCost` is silently blind to it.
- **Solution:** In `scanTranscriptFile`, when a model's resolved pricing is *unknown* — no positive per-token rate and no tiered pricing — and the turn actually burned tokens, drop the transport's fabricated `$0` and surface the turn as a missing-cost entry instead of recording a confident `$0`.
- **What changed:** `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` — new `isModelPricingKnown()` helper and a sibling branch in `scanTranscriptFile` that mirrors the existing tiered-pricing override. `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` — regression test.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Models with positive or tiered pricing are unaffected; entries with zero token usage are not flagged; a transport-recorded *positive* cost is preserved; recorded costs for priced models are untouched. No change to model-catalog generation or the gateway pricing cache.

## Motivation

A freshly-onboarded, idle host on `2026.5.20` (default `openai/gpt-5.5`, `api_key` auth) accrued ~1.78M tokens/day, yet `usage-cost` reported `$0` with `missingCostEntries: 0`, so budget/spike monitoring never fired. Surfacing unknown-priced usage as *missing* lets downstream tooling tell "genuinely free" apart from "price unknown" instead of trusting a false `$0`. Tracks #85858.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85858
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `usage-cost` reports a confident `$0` (`missingCostEntries: 0`) for unpriced `gpt-5.5` usage, hiding real spend from budget/spike monitoring.
- Real environment tested: self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` on Linux; default model `openai/gpt-5.5`; `api_key` auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent change to the installed build, restarted the gateway, then ran `openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json --expect-final` over the same already-logged usage.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output of the `openclaw gateway usage-cost` command — before vs. after the patch, same logs:

```
BEFORE (shipped build) -- openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 0 }

AFTER (patched build) -- same command, same already-logged usage
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 32 }
```

- Observed result after fix: the same 1,780,235 tokens of otherwise-invisible unpriced usage are now surfaced as 32 missing-cost entries; `totalCost` stays `0` (no fabricated price is invented).
- What was not tested: models configured with *intentional* zero pricing (these would also be surfaced as missing — see scope note below); the macOS/Control-UI cost views.
- Before evidence: included in the terminal capture above.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** codex models ship `cost: {input:0, output:0, cacheRead:0, cacheWrite:0}` in the generated `models.json` because the Codex backend exposes no per-token price. `resolveModelCostConfig` returns that *defined* zero, `estimateUsageCost` computes a confident `0`, and the transport records `cost.total: 0` per turn. Cost aggregation (`applyCostTotal` / `applyCostBreakdown`) only treats `undefined` — not `0` — as missing, so "price unknown" is indistinguishable from "genuinely free."

## Note on the free-vs-unknown distinction

This fix treats an all-zero resolved cost as "unknown" only when the turn has no trustworthy recorded cost (recorded cost is `0`/absent), so a turn that carries a real positive cost is always preserved. A model configured with an *intentional* `0` price would still be surfaced as missing; in practice all-zero catalog costs mean "we don't have a price." A future refinement could add an explicit "free" marker (e.g. `cost: null` vs `cost: 0`) so the two can be told apart; this PR keeps to the minimal, provider-agnostic change that stops the silent `$0`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): add .catch() to fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises
target.body:
## Problem

Three fire-and-forget promises in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` can reject without a `.catch()` handler, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the process:

1. **`schedulePendingLifecycleError`** (line 345): `void completeSubagentRun({...})` without `.catch()`.
2. **`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`** (line 384): Same pattern for timeout-triggered completion.
3. **`ensureListener` event handler** (line 1022): `completeSubagentRun({...})` inside an async IIFE without `.catch()`.

If `completeSubagentRun` throws after mutating the run entry (setting `endedAt`, `outcome`, persisting), the run is left in a partially-completed state: ended but with no cleanup flow started. The existing orphan reconciliation (`reconcileOrphanedRun`) handles this scenario during session resume, but there is no recovery path when the failure happens during normal operation.

## Fix

1. **Add `.catch()` to all three fire-and-forget paths** with `log.warn` for diagnostics.
2. **Schedule two-path catch recovery** in each catch handler via `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId)`. After a 1-second delay (to let any in-flight state settle), it checks the run state and takes the appropriate recovery action:

   - **Path 1 (orphan):** If the child session is gone (`resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` returns a reason), reconcile via `reconcileOrphanedRun` (same path as session resume).
   - **Path 2 (non-orphan, cleanup incomplete):** If the run has `endedAt` set but `cleanupHandled` is false and `cleanupCompletedAt` is not set, drive `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` directly. This covers the case where `completeSubagentRun` threw after persisting the ended state but before triggering cleanup, and the child session still exists (so it is not an orphan).
   - **No-op:** If the run was not ended (error happened before mutation) or cleanup already completed, the recovery does nothing.

Production diff is +46 lines in `subagent-registry.ts`.
## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Fire-and-forget `completeSubagentRun` calls could reject without a handler, causing unhandled promise rejections. If the rejection occurs after persisting `endedAt`/`outcome` but before the cleanup tail, the run is left half-ended with no recovery. The fix adds branch-aware `.catch()` + `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` to all four completion paths: timer error, timer timeout, listener blocked, and listener ok.

Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 24.5.0), Node v26.0.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at commit e0cd9500b1.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Built OpenClaw from patched source and started the gateway

```console
$ node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-23T18:14:10.617-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-23T18:14:12.984-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s)
2026-05-23T18:14:13.321-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-23T18:14:13.322-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins; 2.7s)
```

Step 2. Verified all four branch-aware catch handlers in compiled production bundle

```console
$ BUNDLE=dist/subagent-registry-1ZF808Hw.js
$ grep -n "lifecycle.*completion failed" "$BUNDLE"
1850:log.warn("lifecycle error completion failed", {
1884:log.warn("lifecycle timeout completion failed", {
2320:log.warn("lifecycle blocked completion failed", {
2355:log.warn("lifecycle ok completion failed", {
```

Each completion path (error, timeout, blocked, ok) now logs with a branch-specific message and schedules `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` with its own correct completion params.

Step 3. Verified recovery is scheduled for all branches

```console
$ grep -c "scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery" "$BUNDLE"
5
```

Five calls: one in each of the four catch handlers (error, timeout, blocked, ok) plus the recovery function definition itself.

Evidence after fix: Gateway starts and runs normally. Terminal output from the compiled production bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "lifecycle.*completion failed" dist/subagent-registry-1ZF808Hw.js
1850:log.warn("lifecycle error completion failed"...
1884:log.warn("lifecycle timeout completion failed"...
2320:log.warn("lifecycle blocked completion failed"...
2355:log.warn("lifecycle ok completion failed"...
```

All four completion branches now have branch-aware catch recovery. The blocked branch retries with `status: "error"`, the ok branch retries with `status: "ok"`, matching the existing timer-path pattern.

Observed result after fix: Gateway operational with patched subagent lifecycle handlers. All four completion paths (error, timeout, blocked, ok) schedule `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` with branch-correct params on failure. The outer catch remains as a safety net for unexpected errors in the non-async setup code.

What was not tested: Intentionally crashing `completeSubagentRun` in a live gateway to observe each branch's catch-and-recover path. The recovery mechanism is exercised through the regression test which verifies error state preservation.

target.title: fix(agents): treat aborted subagent runs as terminal [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: subagent lifecycle/wait handling could treat terminal `stopReason: "aborted"` as a successful completion when the older `aborted: true` flag was absent.
- Solution: normalize the canonical aborted stop reason into a terminated error before gateway wait snapshots, run-wait normalization, registry completion, or announcement fallback can report success.
- What changed: added a shared aborted-stop-reason helper and applied it at the gateway, gateway dedupe cache, wait, subagent registry, lifecycle, and announcement-output boundaries.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider, channel, plugin API, config-shape, timeout, retry, or fallback policy changes.

## Motivation

Issue #72293 is a P1 session-state/message-loss bug. An aborted subagent run should not look successful to the parent session, because that can hide an interrupted child run during orchestration and produce an incorrect parent-facing completion state during the beta.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72293
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a real subagent lifecycle `end` event with `stopReason: "aborted"` is no longer converted into an `ok` parent-visible completion.
- Real environment tested: disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm exec tsx /tmp/openclaw-72293-runtime-proof.mjs .
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
OpenClaw aborted subagent lifecycle runtime proof
repoHead=daae39048f62
input.lifecycle.phase=end
input.lifecycle.stopReason=aborted
registry.endedReason=subagent-killed
registry.outcome.status=error
registry.outcome.error=subagent run terminated
parentAnnouncement.outcome.status=error
parentAnnouncement.outcome.error=subagent run terminated
parentAnnouncement.childRunId=proof-aborted-subagent-run
result=TERMINATED_FAILURE
```

- Observed result after fix: the same lifecycle event now becomes `endedReason=subagent-killed`, `status=error`, and the parent announcement carries `subagent run terminated`.
- What was not tested: no live external provider or channel account was used; this issue is in the local gateway/agent/subagent lifecycle path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
OpenClaw aborted subagent lifecycle runtime proof
repoHead=5c4a733912a8
input.lifecycle.phase=end
input.lifecycle.stopReason=aborted
registry.endedReason=subagent-complete
registry.outcome.status=ok
registry.outcome.error=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.outcome.status=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.outcome.error=<missing>
parentAnnouncement.childRunId=<missing>
result=LEGACY_FALSE_SUCCESS
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the live wait/completion path recognized timeout-style `aborted: true`, but did not consistently treat the canonical terminal `stopReason: "aborted"` as non-success.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage existed for persisted killed session state, but not for raw live wait/lifecycle terminal events carrying only `stopReason: "aborted"`.
- Contributing context (if known): session lifecycle persistence already maps the same stop reason to killed, so this was a cross-layer normalization gap.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/run-wait.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `stopReason: "aborted"` cannot pass through as an `ok` wait/subagent completion, including cached gateway dedupe snapshots.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers each boundary where the bad successful state could enter, be cached, or be announced.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): session lifecycle state covered persisted killed mapping, but not the live wait/subagent completion path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Aborted subagent runs are now reported to the parent as terminated failures instead of successful completions.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
lifecycle end stopReason=aborted -> agent.wait ok -> subagent complete -> parent sees success

After:
lifecycle end stopReason=aborted -> agent.wait error -> subagent-killed -> parent sees terminated failure
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable checkout
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated temporary OpenClaw home/config; plugins and control UI disabled

### Steps

1. Register a subagent run through the real subagent registry.
2. Emit real OpenClaw lifecycle events through the agent event bus: `start`, then `end` with `stopReason: "aborted"`.
3. Observe the registry outcome and parent announcement payload.

### Expected

- The run is treated as a terminated failure, with `endedReason: "subagent-killed"` and parent-visible `status: "error"`.

### Actual

- After this patch, matches expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Additional verification on pushed PR head `daae39048f62`:

```text
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
  src/agents/run-wait.test.ts
5 test files passed / 163 tests passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts
11 test files passed / 216 tests passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --pretty false
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --pretty false
passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <13 touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json <13 touched files>
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Note: `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts` is not counted as PR-specific proof because current `origin/main @ 5c4a733912a8` already fails the same 12 nested-completion fixture assertions. The focused announce-output and lifecycle tests above pass on this PR head. Crabbox was not used.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: gateway wait snapshot mapping; cached gateway dedupe snapshots; gateway dedupe waiter wakeup; raw wait normalization; subagent wait completion; direct lifecycle completion; announcement fallback; real registry + event-bus behavior proof for `stopReason: "aborted"`.
- Edge cases checked: existing timeout `aborted: true` behavior remains timeout; yielded runs still pause; blocked liveness remains an error; recoverable wait errors still retry unless the terminal stop reason is aborted.
- What you did **not** verify: live external provider/channel traffic; Crabbox.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: treating every `stopReason: "aborted"` as non-success could affect callers that previously relied on silent success.
  - Mitigation: this matches existing persisted lifecycle semantics for killed runs and only targets the canonical aborted terminal reason.

target.title: fix(gateway): honor restart drain budget for embedded runs
target.body:
Problem

Gateway restarts currently treat active embedded runs differently from active task runs: embedded runs hit a hidden 30s abort grace even when the restart drain budget is configured longer, or explicitly unbounded. That can interrupt legitimate long embedded runs even though operators configured the gateway to wait.

Why this is not superseded by #71465

This is a replacement for #85630, which was closed as duplicate/superseded by #71465. #71465 is related, but it did not remove the embedded-run-specific cap that remains on current `main`.

#71465 established the restart drain path and made the general deferral budget visible, but current `main` still has `RESTART_ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_GRACE_MS = 30_000` and still routes active embedded runs through `resolveActiveRunDrainWaitMs()`. That means embedded runs are still capped at 30 seconds even when the operator-visible restart drain budget is longer or explicitly unbounded. This PR is the focused follow-up for that remaining hidden embedded-run cap.

Change and value

This PR makes active embedded runs use the same restart drain budget as active task runs. It also threads a SIGUSR1 restart intent through the scheduled restart path so a restart that already waited through the pre-restart deferral timeout is treated as forced by the run loop instead of spending the same budget a second time.

Who is affected

Operators using `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`, restart `waitMs`, config reloads that require a gateway restart, or long-running embedded Pi runs during restart.

Who is not affected

This does not change ordinary stop behavior, restart authorization, restart scheduling permissions, or public configuration shape. It only removes the hidden embedded-run cap so embedded runs follow the existing restart drain budget.

Why now

The documented deferral timeout is the restart budget users can see and configure. Keeping a separate hidden embedded-run cap makes the behavior harder to reason about and can discard work sooner than configured.

Implementation

- Removed the separate `RESTART_ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_GRACE_MS` cap.
- Pass `restartDrainTimeoutMs` directly to `waitForActiveEmbeddedRuns`.
- Added in-memory SIGUSR1 restart intent plumbing for scheduled restarts.
- Mark deferral-timeout restarts as `{ force: true }` so the run loop skips a second active-work drain after the deferral budget has already been exhausted.
- Carry forced restart reasons into main-session restart recovery markers.
- Clear the in-flight SIGUSR1 token when a file intent handles an authorized restart signal.

Maintainer decision: restart drain contract

This PR recommends treating the configured restart drain budget as the single restart contract for both active task runs and active embedded runs.

The problem is that #71465 made restart deferral visible and configurable, but the current embedded-run path still applies a separate hidden 30s cap. That creates two conflicting contracts: operators see `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` or restart `waitMs`, while embedded runs can still be aborted earlier. It also makes `0` ambiguous: active tasks can wait indefinitely, but embedded runs cannot.

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Keep the hidden 30s embedded cap | Fast restart recovery; smallest behavior change. | Leaves the visible restart budget misleading; preserves the task/run mismatch; can abort legitimate long embedded work despite a longer or unbounded configured budget. |
| Merge this PR as-is (recommended) | One operator-visible drain budget; embedded runs match active tasks; `0` has one meaning; no new config or docs surface; focused code and tests. | Restarts can wait indefinitely for embedded runs when the operator explicitly configured an unbounded wait. |
| Add an explicit embedded-run watchdog | Keeps an availability escape hatch if maintainers want embedded runs bounded independently. | Adds a second public policy/setting to document and support; `waitMs: 0` no longer means fully unbounded for all active work; still needs a maintainer-chosen default. |
| Split only the deferral-timeout force fix | Avoids double-spending the restart budget after a deferral timeout. | Leaves the hidden 30s embedded cap and the main operator-facing bug unresolved. |

Recommended approach: merge #85708 as-is, subject to normal CI and maintainer review. The operator has already chosen the restart budget; if they choose a longer or unbounded wait, embedded runs should follow that same contract. If maintainers want a hard embedded-run watchdog, it should be a separate explicit product decision rather than an undocumented cap that silently overrides the visible restart budget.

Risk, CI, and security disposition

- Compatibility: This intentionally changes behavior for any setup that unknowingly depended on the hidden 30s embedded-run abort cap. That is the compatibility risk. The recommended resolution is to make the documented restart budget the source of truth; if maintainers want a separate embedded-run maximum, it should be explicit and tested rather than hidden.
- Availability: With `waitMs: 0` or `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs: 0`, an active embedded run can keep restart drain open indefinitely. This matches the existing unbounded-wait contract for active task runs and is acceptable when the operator explicitly configured an unbounded restart wait. The explicit watchdog option remains available if maintainers prefer a hard maximum.
- CI: ClawSweeper's earlier pending-check note has been resolved for the current head. Current replacement head `c3020da0d065` has completed the visible GitHub workflow runs successfully: Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep - PR Diff, CodeQL, CodeQL Critical Quality, and CI. Future reruns should still be allowed to finish before merge.
- Security: No additional security fix is needed from the ClawSweeper note. The diff changes in-process gateway restart control flow and tests only; it does not touch dependencies, lockfiles, CI definitions, secret handling, or package supply-chain surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Active embedded runs now honor the configured restart drain budget instead of a hidden 30s cap, and deferral-timeout SIGUSR1 restarts skip a second drain.
- Real environment tested: Local contributor worktree on replacement head `2c0889f7f1`; the branch was created from `origin/main` `55a0c9b1f4` and GitHub reports the PR mergeable against current base `4ec85762ab`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```sh
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/restart.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/lifecycle.runtime.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts
  git diff --check HEAD^..HEAD
  node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-restart-drain-proof.mjs
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-restart-drain-proof.mjs` exercised real `runGatewayLoop`, `ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUNS`, `emitGatewayRestart`, and `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle` paths and printed this after-fix output:

  ```json
  {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "startCount": 3,
    "waitZero": {
      "drainedWithoutAbort": true,
      "close": {
        "reason": "gateway restarting",
        "restartExpectedMs": 1500,
        "drainTimeoutMs": 15000
      }
    },
    "deferralTimeout": {
      "abortCount": 1,
      "close": {
        "reason": "gateway restarting",
        "restartExpectedMs": 1500,
        "drainTimeoutMs": 0
      }
    },
    "eventKinds": [
      "start",
      "embedded.drained",
      "close",
      "start",
      "embedded.abort",
      "close",
      "start",
      "close",
      "exit"
    ]
  }
  ```

  Targeted exact-head checks also passed: CLI restart loop suite, 35 tests; infra restart suites, 34 tests; gateway reload suite, 11 tests; formatting check for all 8 changed files; `git diff --check HEAD^..HEAD`.
- Observed result after fix: The proof showed an explicit unbounded restart wait draining an active embedded run without aborting it, and a deferral-timeout restart aborting once with `drainTimeoutMs: 0` instead of spending a second active-work drain budget.
- What was not tested: No changed-path proof gap remains. #85630, which carried the same scoped diff before replacement, completed remote GCP/Crabbox validation, multi-lane review, Claude `--print`, Codex review, and GitHub CI before it was closed as superseded. Current replacement head `c3020da0d065` has completed the visible GitHub workflow runs successfully.

Review gates

- Same scoped diff as #85630.
- #85630 completed multi-lane read-only review; accepted findings were fixed or explicitly classified.
- #85630 completed Claude `--print` multi-lane review after increasing timeout to 20 minutes per lane; no blockers remained.
- #85630 completed Codex review with no discrete actionable bugs.
- Replacement head reran targeted local checks and real behavior proof after cherry-picking onto a fresh main worktree.
- Current replacement head `c3020da0d065` completed visible GitHub workflow runs successfully: Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep - PR Diff, CodeQL, CodeQL Critical Quality, and CI.

Changelog

No changelog entry added; leaving release-note/changelog ownership to maintainers.

target.title: fix(codex): stop persisting per-turn OpenClaw skills snapshot in Codex native user history (#84662)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Codex app-server (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`) wraps the OpenClaw **skills snapshot** into the per-turn user-input text via `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` and `prependCodexOpenClawPromptContext`. Because `turn/start.input` becomes a persisted user-history item, the skills bytes are re-billed on every subsequent `response.create.input`, growing the model input by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause).
- **Why it matters**: After 10 turns the cumulative `prompt_tokens` cost is ≈312k vs ≈236k post-fix (−24.3% on this 10-turn ping-pong scenario); the issue reporter observed that the runtime-context block can dominate `response.create.input` over a longer session.
- **What changed**: Route the OpenClaw **skills snapshot only** through `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` (the same field cron and heartbeat triggers already use in production), via a new `openClawSkillsPrompt` option threaded through `buildTurnStartParams` → `buildTurnCollaborationMode` → `buildTurnScopedCollaborationInstructions`. `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` now emits the workspace section only and its per-turn wrapper is renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` so the user-input lane is correctly labelled.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: User-editable **workspace context** (incl. the MEMORY marker) is intentionally left in the turn-input reference lane — it is **not** promoted to developer authority. A unit-test invariant pins this (`thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions"). Workspace bootstrap (#77372), AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, cron payload guidance, the PI runner `openclaw.runtime-context` path, and the subagent / ACP / CLI surfaces are untouched. **Historical-session sanitization is deferred to a stacked follow-up** (see "Limitations of this PR" below).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations  (codex app-server `turn/start` shape)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses the skills component of #84662. This PR routes future skills snapshots through `developer_instructions` so they are not appended to Codex native user history going forward. It does **not** implement the issue's *safe interim* historical sanitizer for already-bound threads; that half is deferred to a stacked follow-up (see "Limitations of this PR").
- Related: supersedes closed #84790 (TurboTheTurtle, closed 2026-05-22) by narrowing scope to the skills snapshot only — workspace/MEMORY stays in the turn-input reference lane that #84790's [P1] prompt-authority finding was raised against.
- Related: complements merged #83454 (Eva, 2026-05-18) — skills are treated as **stable OpenClaw plugin/tool guidance** and routed through `developer_instructions`, while user-editable workspace text **remains user-reference scoped in turn input** per #83454. This is not a reversal of #83454: the user-editable surface that #83454 protected is left where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned plugin-manifest surface moves, joining the workspace bootstrap files (#77372) that already live in `developer_instructions`.
- Related: builds on merged #77372 (lonexreb, workspace bootstrap → `developer_instructions`) as the lane-precedent for OpenClaw-owned stable instructions.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: The shared per-turn wrapper `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` conflates two fragments with different authority + lifetime needs:
  1. **Skills snapshot** (`params.skillsSnapshot?.prompt`) — OpenClaw-owned plugin manifest text, byte-stable across a session, **not** editable from chat/MEMORY. Analogous to the workspace bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) that already live in `developer_instructions` via #77372.
  2. **Workspace prompt context** (`workspaceBootstrapContext.promptContext`) — **user-editable** workspace files (e.g., `MEMORY.md`, custom project docs). Per #84662 §Security/rank caution, this must remain quoted/reference scoped.
  Bundling both into `turn/start.input[0].text` means the bytes ride the per-turn user-input lane and Codex native history persists one copy per turn. Issue #84662 carves these two halves apart explicitly; the wider scope from #84790 collapsed them back together and ran into the prompt-authority blocker raised in that review.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No unit test pinned the authority boundary (workspace user-editable text must not appear in `developer_instructions`). This PR adds one (`thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions").
- **Contributing context**: cron and heartbeat triggers already populate `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` in production. The generated `@openai/codex` 0.132.0 `v2/TurnStartParams.ts` does not currently declare `collaborationMode` (the formal basis for #84790's [P1] protocol concern), but the bundled runtime binary parses and honors the field on cron / heartbeat triggers today — empirical evidence below shows it also honors it on the chat trigger.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts` (6 new cases) + `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (1 modified case at the existing turn-input assertion).
- Scenario the test should lock in: (a) chat trigger renders skills as `developer_instructions`; (b) chat trigger without skills returns `null` `developer_instructions`; (c) cron composes cron + skills; (d) heartbeat composes heartbeat + overlay + skills; (e) cron without skills is byte-identical to current main; (f) workspace user-editable context never reaches `developer_instructions` (authority invariant).
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is structural (wrong lane); the structural invariant pins both halves (skills present in `developer_instructions`, workspace absent from it) without needing live-model evidence to rerun on every CI tick.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **Per-turn token billing on the default `main` agent** (new turns after this PR ships): skills bytes no longer re-bill in every `response.create.input`; the cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` cost drops by ~24% on the ping-pong scenario in the Real-behavior proof. Workspace-context bytes continue to re-bill at the same rate as before (~+540/turn floor).
- **Per-turn user-input wrapper text** (new turns): renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:\n…` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:\n…`. The block now contains only the `## OpenClaw Workspace Context` section; the `## OpenClaw Skills` section is gone (moved to `developer_instructions`).
- **Existing Codex native threads bound before this PR ships**: see "Limitations of this PR" below.
- **Snapshot fixtures**: three `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md` files regenerated to reflect the new wrapper text and `userInputText.chars` (≈ +15 chars from the wording change; the 6,557-char skills section that previously sat inside the user input is no longer included in the snapshot of `turn/start.input`).

## Limitations of this PR (deferred half explicitly disclosed)

This PR **prevents future skills bytes from being appended** to Codex native user history. It does **not** sanitize history for threads that were bound before the fix ships.

- **Existing bound Codex native threads** that already contain `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items from prior turns will continue to replay those items on `response.create.input` until either (a) the thread cycles to a fresh native session, or (b) the deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer (issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix") lands as a stacked follow-up PR that strips the wrapper from `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages`.
- The snapshot-fixture check (`workspaceBootstrapContextFrom` field in `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md`) verifies the **current projection shape** for new turns; it is **not** a regression guard for the deferred sanitizer half — old mirrored-history user items are out of its scope.
- The 10-turn benchmark below was run with fresh user IDs (i.e., fresh Codex native sessions), which is why the patched arm's per-turn `prompt_tokens` curve flattens immediately. Users on long-lived bound threads will see the curve flatten only after the next thread reset, or after the follow-up sanitizer lands.

The deferred follow-up will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle` (PR #84790's sanitizer logic) and is independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.

## Authority safety (why this is not the #84790 design)

| | PR #84790 (CLOSED 2026-05-22) | This PR |
|---|---|---|
| Content moved into `developer_instructions` | skills + workspace + MEMORY | **skills only** |
| Workspace / MEMORY routing | through `developer_instructions` (with a disclaimer wrapper) | **unchanged from current `main`** — stays in turn-input reference lane established by #83454 |
| Session-history sanitization | bundled in same PR | **deferred follow-up** (disclosed above) |
| Protocol-acceptance argument | schema inspection only | schema + production cron / heartbeat parity + wire-level Codex native rollout + cacheRead ratio uplift on the same scenario |
| Authority invariant test | n/a | `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` pins workspace text never appears in `developer_instructions` |

Reading order:

- Issue #84662 §"Security/rank caution": *"Stable instructions such as OpenClaw skills may be safe to move to developer/bootstrap instructions, but user-editable workspace context should remain quoted/reference scoped."* This PR follows that caveat literally.
- PR #84790 was closed by its author after the wider scope failed the prompt-authority finding raised in that review (wrapper text does not actually downgrade message authority).
- PR #83454 deliberately moved both halves *out* of `developer_instructions` so user-editable text would not gain developer authority. This PR complements that policy: workspace text stays where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned plugin-manifest skills snapshot — a stable, OpenClaw-controlled surface analogous to the bootstrap files in #77372 — moves to `developer_instructions`.
- PR #77372 already routes workspace-bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) through `developer_instructions`; skills join their natural peers under this PR.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Codex app-server persists the OpenClaw skills snapshot as a per-turn user-history item in native Codex threads, causing the model's `response.create.input` to grow by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause). After this fix the skills snapshot rides `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` once per thread and is not replayed for **new turns on threads opened after this PR ships**.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw checkout at the upstream/main HEAD this PR is based on, rebuilt dist; live OpenAI Codex backend (`openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`); two local OpenAI-compatible OpenClaw gateways on separate ports sharing the same eval state directory (one unpatched baseline, one with this PR's patch applied); a live 10-turn ping-pong benchmark as the proof surface. A separate live runtime instance on an unrelated port was observed before / during / after the experiment via `/healthz` to confirm no PID change in unrelated processes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- Start two OpenClaw gateways on separate ports against an eval `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, one built from current upstream/main (arm A — baseline) and one built from this PR's branch (used for arms A' and B), each launched via `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway` with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- Drive a 10-turn `body_contains: "pong"` ping-pong scenario against each gateway via an OpenAI-compatible benchmark CLI, using a distinct user ID per arm (forces a fresh Codex native session per arm) for three arms:
  - Arm A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state), default `main` agent.
  - Arm A' — patched, default `main` agent.
  - Arm B — patched gateway, but a separately-configured agent with `tools.profile=minimal`, `systemPromptOverride="."`, `skills=[]` (i.e., a no-skills control).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/{run-attempt,thread-lifecycle,run-attempt.context-engine}.test.ts --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`

**Evidence after fix (10-turn live benchmark × 3 arms, all 30/30 functional `body_contains: "pong"`)**:

| Arm | growth/turn | turn 1 / turn 10 prompt_tokens | cum 10t prompt_tokens | cacheRead/input (turn 9 steady) | uncached input tokens (turn 9) | skills.count / promptChars |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state) | +2,194 | 21,286 / 41,032 | 311,590 | 93.27 % | 2,614 | 20 / 6,629 |
| A' — patched | **+535** | 21,166 / 25,981 | **235,735** | **96.08 %** | **998** | 20 / 6,557 |
| B — no-skills control (patched gateway, no-skills agent) | +540 | 19,552 / 24,412 | 219,820 | 95.98 % | 960 | 0 / 0 |

All three arms used **fresh** user IDs so each arm opened a new Codex native session — the patched-arm curve reflects the new-thread behavior, not the legacy-thread replay floor. Per the "Limitations" section above, legacy bound threads are out of scope for this proof.

- Per-turn growth on the default agent drops from +2,194 to +535 tokens/turn (**−75.6 %**); cumulative 10-turn save = **−75,855 tokens (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline.
- Arm B's no-skills floor (+540/turn) is within ±5 tokens/turn of arm A' — confirming the +535/turn residual on the patched arm is the workspace-context replay floor, **not** skills. This PR intentionally leaves that floor in place per #84662 §Security/rank caution; reducing it requires either the deferred history sanitizer or a separate workspace-routing decision (out of scope).
- Codex native rollout for the patched arm shows `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` populated with a **6,740-char byte-stable** payload (md5 `fc5d0370` across all 10 turns) starting `OpenClaw skills available for this turn:\nThese skills describe OpenClaw plugin capabilities…\n\n## OpenClaw Skills\n…`. Byte-stability is the precondition for the OpenAI prefix cache; the patched arm reaches the same **96.08 %** steady-state cacheRead at turn 9 as the no-skills control (95.98 %), a **+2.81 pp** uplift over baseline at the same turn index.
- Workspace context bytes (including the MEMORY marker) appear only in the per-turn user message under the renamed wrapper `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` and never in `developer_instructions` — verified both at the wire level (Codex native rollout) and as a unit-test structural invariant.

**Latency**: trimmed mean **−448 ms (−8.8 %)** on the patched arm vs the baseline arm (5,102 → 4,654 ms after dropping hi/lo across n=10), both arms dominated by a single turn-1 cold-start outlier (A: 26,862 ms, A': 22,453 ms). No functional regression. We do **not** claim latency improvement at n=10; a 50-turn rerun would be the right place to make a stronger latency claim.

**Observed result after fix**: per-turn `prompt_tokens` growth on the default `main` agent drops from **+2,194 → +535 tokens/turn** (−75.6 %), within ±5 tokens/turn of the no-skills control (+540). Cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` saves **−75,855 (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline. 30 / 30 functional `body_contains: "pong"` success across the 3 arms. `developer_instructions` is byte-stable across all 10 turns on the patched arm (6,740 chars, md5 `fc5d0370`); workspace / MEMORY text never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified at the wire level and as a unit-test structural invariant). No functional regression observed.

**What was not tested**:

- Long-session 50 / 100-turn scaling under this change (deferred to a follow-up long-session validation; the +535/turn residual rate predicts a ~107k cumulative save at 50 turns versus the unpatched curve, but this is unmeasured here).
- Workspace-file mid-session edit cache-bust behaviour (workspace was static across the ~30 s experiment).
- Out-of-sample models (only `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini` was exercised).
- Live Discord / Telegram production-channel turn (the OpenAI-compatible gateway was the proof surface, not a channel).
- **Legacy bound Codex native threads** that contain pre-fix `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items — by design (see "Limitations of this PR"). The deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer is what makes those threads stop replaying historical wrappers.
- Session-history sanitization itself, including the `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing path — out of scope for this PR (deferred to stacked follow-up).
- `skills.promptChars` drifted by 72 chars between the two arms (6,629 vs 6,557) due to a bundled-plugin asset rebuild difference between the two checkouts, not the source patch; `skills.entries.count` is **20 / 20** in both arms and the same skill names appear in both arms' Codex native rollouts.
- A single turn-10 OpenAI cache-eviction anomaly on the patched arm reported `cached_input_tokens=10,624` (turn-1-like) and `uncached=15,357` against the byte-stable `developer_instructions` payload (md5 stable); turns 2–9 each showed ~96.1 % cache rate and the steady-state quoted above uses turn 9. The headline per-turn growth and cumulative `prompt_tokens` metrics are unaffected (they count both cached and uncached input). A repeat run 12 hours earlier on a prior upstream/main base reported 96.07 % at the same turn-10 index, so the turn-10 dip is a one-off platform-side event, not a behavior of the patch.

## Validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
```

## Follow-up planned (separate stacked PR)

Session-history sanitizer for already-bound Codex native threads that already carry legacy `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items. Implements `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing per issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix". Builds on the sanitizer logic PR #84790 contributed; will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle`. Independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.

target.title: refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- rename the former Pi agent runtime/package surfaces to OpenClaw-owned agent runtime names
- internalize the runtime packages into core and wire the embedded OpenClaw runtime through the current plugin/runtime contracts
- remove copied session-export HTML assets, package-manager install/update leftovers, runtime `.pi` auth discovery, and stale internal Pi naming
- split modern agent runtime id helpers from deprecated Pi runtime-id compatibility; keep compatibility in explicit doctor/session/env/plugin-boundary helpers
- keep deprecated plugin SDK compatibility only at named boundaries, including `resolveLegacyAgentAccessToken`
- add the Pi third-party notice for incorporated code

## Verification

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer has live core/runtime paths depending on Pi package names or `.pi` credential discovery; remaining Pi runtime-id compatibility is isolated in deprecated compat helpers for shipped config/session/env values and plugin SDK boundary compatibility.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/aliases.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/backend.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>`; `git diff --check`; `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ... corepack pnpm check:changed`.

Observed result after fix: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runtime.ts` is gone; modern runtime-id helpers live in `src/agents/agent-runtime-id.ts`; deprecated Pi alias handling is quarantined in `src/agents/deprecated-agent-runtime-compat.ts`; plugin SDK `resolveEmbeddedAgentRuntime` remains exported.

target.title: fix(update): ignore restart script spawn failures

- Make the detached gateway restart script handoff best-effort when `spawn()` fails immediately.
- Attach a child `error` listener so ENOENT/EACCES-style spawn failures after handoff do not crash the update CLI.

Fixes #83892.
